name: dbt Cloud MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official dbt Cloud MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with dbt's analytics engineering platform — querying model metadata, running dbt jobs, checking job status, exploring data lineage, and managing dbt Cloud environments.
use_cases: Querying dbt model metadata and data lineage from analytics agents
Triggering dbt jobs for pipeline orchestration from agents
Checking dbt job run status and failure analysis
Exploring semantic layer definitions for AI-driven data querying
Validating dbt model dependencies before deployment
not_for: Teams not using dbt for data transformation
Direct database operations (use database-specific MCPs for that)
best_when: An agent needs to interact with dbt models, run transformations, or query the semantic layer in a dbt Cloud environment.
avoid_when: You're using Spark, Flink, or other non-SQL transformation tools.

auth.notes: Service tokens with account, project, or job-level permission scopes. Personal API tokens for individual access. Service tokens recommended for agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer plan: free for 1 developer seat, limited cloud features
pricing.notes: dbt Core is free open source. dbt Cloud adds managed runs, CI/CD, documentation. API access on Team and Enterprise.

known_agent_gotchas: Account ID + project ID required for most operations — configure both
Job runs are async — agents must poll for completion
dbt semantic layer requires separate configuration and understanding of metrics
API v2 vs v3 endpoints have different capabilities — check version compatibility
Model and job IDs are numeric — agents must discover IDs before operating
Service token permission scopes control what operations are allowed per project
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Service tokens with project-level permissions provide good granularity. SOC 2 Type II, GDPR. Enterprise-grade analytics platform security.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Service tokens with project-level scopes. SOC 2 Type II, GDPR. Good analytics platform security.
